mnemonics: Global Allocation is Greedy and Global (can take frames from any process), Local Allocation is Limited and Local (can only use its own allotted frames).
keyPoints: The three main frame allocation schemes are equal allocation, proportional allocation, and priority allocation.
Global replacement allows a process to select a replacement frame from the set of all frames, even if that frame currently belongs to another process.
Local replacement requires that each process selects replacement frames only from its own currently allocated set.
commonMistakes: Assuming that global replacement is always better than local; it can lead to a process being unable to control its own page-fault rate (thrashing).
Believing that proportional allocation based on process size is always fair; it doesn't account for the process's priority or behavior.
